response: to find the equilibrium population size we set the rate of change to zero meaning the growth equals the harvesting loss so we solve the equation 0.1P(1 - P/1000) = 10 + 5sin(pi t/6) which shows that the equilibrium P_e is not a single constant value but fluctuates with time due to the sine term in the harvesting function however if we consider the average harvesting rate of 10 we can estimate the average equilibrium by solving 0.1P(1 - P/1000) = 10 which yields a quadratic equation with solutions indicating the population stabilizes around a specific range before the carrying capacity changes for the second part where K decreases linearly as K(t) = 1000 - 3t the equilibrium condition becomes 0.1P(1 - P/(1000 - 3t)) = 10 + 5sin(pi t/6) and the population becomes unsustainable when the maximum possible growth cannot offset the minimum harvesting or when the carrying capacity drops so low that no positive real solution exists for P this critical moment occurs roughly when the carrying capacity approaches the threshold where the intrinsic growth rate times the population cannot exceed the harvesting floor of 10 turtles per year specifically when K(t) falls below the value required to support the minimum harvest which happens when 1000 - 3t is small enough that the logistic term cannot generate 10 turtles even at optimal density solving for the point where the discriminant of the equilibrium quadratic becomes negative or where P_e becomes zero or imaginary indicates the collapse time which is approximately when K(t) drops to a level where 0.1 * (K/4) < 10 meaning K < 400 so 1000 - 3t = 400 gives 3t = 600 and t = 200 years as the time when the population can no longer sustain itself under the worst case scenario of constant minimum harvest level and the word level is a palindrome in your response
